authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal construction safety regulator. This standard requires accident-prevention programs and frequent and regular inspections by competent persons, so a changed safety condition must block punch-list progression.
used_for: refusal when safety conditions change the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Work Permits Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an authority having jurisdiction. Its official guide states when permits are required, who may obtain them, and how signoff remains with the permit process and permit holder rather than a punch-list lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Building Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official final-inspection quick sheet gives a concrete public baseline for approved plans, associated permits, special-inspection letters, and test reports required before final inspection and permit completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Kahua QRG Punch Lists
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA publishes this official quick reference for an active public-owner punch-list system. It is directly authoritative for item assignment, status changes, attachments, and export workflow in a commercial construction context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Punch-item intake, assignment, and lifecycle progression

authoritative_sources.title: eRETA and Customer Letters
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official completion and closeout letter guidance distinguishes substantial completion, contract completion, and closeout package completion, which constrains what the lane can treat as complete versus owner-accepted.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Completion posture and owner handoff review

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents: Process, Contents, and Acceptance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public building guidance platform. This reference defines issues logs, systems manuals, test reports, training records, and final commissioning reports that directly constrain completion-evidence review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Closeout evidence and turnover package review
used_for: systems manual and training record expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Roles and Responsibilities in the Commissioning Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized public workflow reference names contractor, subcontractor, owner, and commissioning-party responsibilities for issue logs, system checklists, systems manuals, and commissioning reports, which prevents the lane from implying acceptance authority it does not own.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Trade responsibility and turnover ownership review

authoritative_sources.title: Operations & Maintenance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APPA is a recognized professional body for facilities operations and maintenance. Its public reference anchors work identification, workflow, controls, and information-system terminology relevant to work-order and corrective-work handling.

workflow_stages.name: Punch-item intake and readiness normalization
workflow_stages.description: Classify incoming punch items, normalize deficiency, location, trade, permit, and due-date context, and decide whether the item is ready to enter controlled execution.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Corrective-work progression and lifecycle control
workflow_stages.description: Move items through open, in-progress, ready-for-backcheck, rework, blocked, and closeout-ready states using named evidence and explicit next-owner expectations.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Permit, inspection, and safety gating
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether corrective work or closure can proceed based on permit status, approved plans, inspection outcomes, safety posture, and special-inspection or testing dependencies.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and owner handoff packaging
workflow_stages.description: Review photos, backcheck notes, issue logs, systems manuals, training records, and turnover artifacts before preparing completion or escalation outputs.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: permits, approved plans, and permit signoff records
inspection results, correction notices, and special-inspection letters
site logs, safety observations, stop-work notices, and access records
photos, videos, marked-up drawings, and backcheck notes
commissioning issues logs, test reports, systems manuals, and training records
owner-turnover checklists, closeout packages, and acceptance correspondence

allowed: Classify punch-list requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Advance, hold, return for rework, or recommend closeout-ready posture using documented lifecycle state and evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, completion evidence records, exception summaries, and escalation notes tied to named records.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist or approver when permit, inspection, safety, design, or trade authority shifts.
escalate: Permit, inspection, or safety conditions materially change whether corrective work or closure may proceed.
Public baseline sources, tenant policy, or contract terms conflict on completion, turnover, or acceptance requirements.
Requests require licensed trade judgment, code interpretation, engineer-of-record review, or final owner acceptance authority.
refuse: Mark work complete without completion evidence, backcheck traceability, or required turnover artifacts.
Claim permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, or final acceptance authority.
Override a safety stop condition, failed inspection, or unresolved closeout dependency to preserve schedule.

domain_failure_modes: closing punch items based on verbal field confirmation while photos, backcheck notes, or inspection records are missing
treating substantial completion as final closeout even though turnover documents, commissioning evidence, or owner acceptance remain incomplete
allowing schedule pressure to override a new safety observation or failed inspection
guessing trade ownership, permit status, or completion authority when tenant systems of record are not named
collapsing punch-list handling into generic issue tracking without documenting permit, inspection, subcontractor, and closeout dependencies
acting as code interpreter, licensed trade signoff owner, or final commercial approver instead of escalating

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Substantial completion versus final completion naming
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public-owner and jurisdictional sources use related but not identical milestone names for substantial completion, contract completion, and closeout completion. The pack treats tenant contract terms and owner acceptance rules as runtime controls and does not guess milestone equivalence.
